title: Contract referrers can bypass the EOA-only gate during construction
reason: The constructor-time `extcodesize == 0` bypass is real in isolation, but this contract never uses referral state for payouts, permissions, or fund flows. On-chain impact is limited to metadata integrity, so the reported protocol harm is too speculative to keep as a reportable finding.

title: Referral eligibility can be claimed retroactively after prior participation
reason: `processReferrals()` only records referral relationships and counts; no reward-distribution or economic logic consumes that state anywhere in the codebase. Without a concrete on-chain value flow tied to referrals, this remains a low-value bookkeeping issue rather than realistic protocol-level harm.
